summary: Set 069 Session 4 of 6: the Podman model-authored-probe lane (proposal rung b) -- the ONE lane where the model AUTHORS the probe body, so it runs ONLY inside a real Podman container (the container is the SECURITY BOUNDARY, not the floor). GATED on the Podman feasibility spike, which is GREEN (docs/proposals/2026-06-16-pull-architecture-capabilities/podman-spike/spike-result.json: 6/6 acceptance criteria, podman 4.9.3) -> S4 PROCEEDS with the full lane (NOT the NO-GO path). Ran on claude-opus-4-8 high (operator launch; the routed rec was sonnet-4-6/medium). ALL ADDITIVE -- absent a PodmanLaneConfig the loop/producer/verdict-schema are byte-for-byte the prior (S3) behavior. SHIPPED CODE. NEW MODULE ai_router/podman_sandbox.py (run_test_sandbox's sibling; isolation by `podman run` instead of `git worktree add`, built on the shared run_subprocess_capped): the `podman run` cage (--rm, lane LABEL, --network=none, --read-only, --cap-drop=ALL, --security-opt=no-new-privileges, repo bind-mounted READ-ONLY at /repo, tmpfs /scratch as the only writable place, hard wall-clock timeout with CRASH-SAFE force-remove-by-name teardown); PodmanCaps + podman_caps_from_config; image_is_digest_pinned + build_image (one-time, never per-probe) + DEFAULT_IMAGE_TAG + CONTAINERFILE_DIR; podman_footprint (LANE-LOCAL by label: 0 leftover labeled containers + 0 labeled volumes + the reused image present); build_probe_argv wraps the model body as `python -B -c <body>` (NOT -I/-E, which would drop PYTHONPATH=/repo set on the podman argv so a probe imports the code under review and drives a REAL public entrypoint). THE THREE SPIKE FINDINGS CARRIED: (1) cgroup_caps_enforceable() detects cgroup v2 and emits --memory/--pids-limit/--cpus ONLY when honored (rootless cgroups-v1 ignores them; the wall-clock cap is the load-bearing bound there); (2) the timed-out --rm container is force-removed by name (the ~10s rootless-WSL path; a killed `podman run` client can leave the container alive, so the rm is required not redundant); (3) stdout (probe_output, the replay-hashed bytes) is SEPARATED from stderr (runtime_diagnostics, never hashed) so a podman runtime warning cannot perturb a replay match. NEW ai_router/podman/Containerfile (operator-authored, no-secrets, tag base for dev/CI with a digest-pin deploy step documented). ai_router/pull_verifier.py: RUN_AUTHORED_PROBE_TOOL + _run_authored_probe_tool_schema (probe body + entrypointRef/entrypointKind for the meta-oracle + claim for severity-gating) + _dispatch_run_authored_probe (cage dispatch OUTSIDE the byte-equality guard; validates entrypoint kind in PUBLIC_ENTRYPOINT_KINDS, runs default_triage, runs the cage, on a reproduced run does a SECOND-container pristine replay -> replay_matched re-runnability/flake signal surfaced to the agent+human, and prepends a NOTE when the image is not digest-pinned); PodmanLaneConfig + default_triage (TRIAGE-ONLY: rejects empty body / missing claim / no-ai_router-reference [meta-oracle], ESCALATES network/subprocess [a rung-c escape], NEVER approves -- the container makes approval unnecessary); _Execution gains kind=authored + probe_id/probe_body/replay_matched (match_id -> probe_id); _verdict_tool_schema(allow_authored_evidence) adds a gated probeId field. THE CENTRAL SAFETY PROPERTY (gpt-5-4 confirmed PASS): a model-authored probe can NEVER mint REPRODUCED -- _build_transcript returns None for kind=authored (validate_transcript requires a TRUSTED commandId XOR templateId, never model-authored argv), and _stamp_evidence_tiers CAPS an authored-backed finding (a probeId match OR a sole authored execution) at HYPOTHESIS (a REPRODUCED claim is DOWNGRADED to a flagged container-backed suspicion, not collapsed to a bare read-claim); by_probe is lane-keyed so a probeId never cross-binds a command/template lane. Promotion to the floor is the S5 human-gated ratchet (the human stays the meta-oracle defense -- a deliberate S4 non-goal to retire the manual run). ai_router/pull_critique.py: produce_path_aware_critique threads podman_lane_config to pull_route (+ it counts as an execution lane for blast-radius-budgeted caps); _build_exec_configs + --podman-lane/--podman-image CLI (the read-only bind mount means it does NOT require --exec-ref; ref is provenance only). ai_router/router-config.yaml: a pull_verifier.podman.caps block (wall_seconds 30 the load-bearing bound; memory/pids/cpus cgroup-v2-only; enforce_resource_caps omitted -> auto-detect). DOC: ai_router/docs/pull-verifier.md gains a 'What Set 069 S4 added' section. VERIFICATION (cross-provider, gpt-5-4, gated->REQUIRED -- routed_gate trips on blast-radius + breadth [9 files] + build-ci-config [router-config.yaml]; up-front conventions block per L-064-10). R1 NEEDS_FIX with the CENTRAL SAFETY CLAIM already PASS + 4 findings: (1) Containerfile base not digest-pinned / doc story aspirational -> made un-pinned VISIBLE at runtime (a NOTE prepended when image_is_digest_pinned is False) + the checked-in tag + deploy-time pin documented honestly (a base digest is arch-specific, deliberately not hard-coded in a portable template); (2) footprint not lane-local on a shared host (containers by prefix, volumes/images global) -> every container carries a queryable lane LABEL and podman_footprint filters ps + volume ls by it, replacing the brittle global image-count with `podman image exists <ref>` (the lane never builds an image at runtime); (3) replay_matched=False still rendered 'reproduced the same output' -> branch-correct text; (4) no direct test of build_probe_argv's -B/no-I/no-E contract -> added. R2 PASS: all 4 RESOLVED, central safety claim PASS (unchanged), no regressions, no new defects. Full ai_router suite GREEN: 1856 passed / 5 skipped (baseline 1820/1 + 36 new passes + 4 new REAL-PODMAN regressions [network/read-only+no-leak/timeout-teardown/disk-footprint] that require a real podman + a built image so they run on Linux CI / WSL and SKIP on the Windows host -- by design). Metered verification ~$0.784 (R1 $0.424 + R2 $0.359) + next-session rec $0.003. NO release this session (PyPI is S6); ai_router version unchanged; NO Marketplace/extension change (spec non-goal: no UI this whole set). DEFERRED to later sessions: the ceiling->floor ratchet + measured replacement gate (S5), the synthesis-doc update + release + dogfood (S6). Next-session rec routed (next-session-rec.md): anthropic/opus-4-8/medium for S5's design-heavy ratchet + benchmark-gate work (the analysis model returned a stale opus model name, mapped to the closest current candidate; prioritizes architectural soundness / low rework over raw token cost).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (next-session-rec.md) recommends anthropic/opus-4-8 medium for S5 (ceiling->floor ratchet + measured replacement gate): a design-heavy task with real solution-variance (what makes a good candidate-falsifier admission gate; how to pre-register an honest small-n benchmark; the telemetry schema) where architectural soundness minimizes high-cost rework, so a top-tier reasoning engine is the cheapest-CAPABLE choice on dollars+rework (it returned a stale opus model name, mapped to the closest current candidate). Operator may instead pick codex gpt-5.4/high or sonnet-4-6/high; cross-provider verification stays REQUIRED (the diff will trip routed_gate). No release (that is S6).
